title: Queen Of Jordan Ii: Mystery Of The Phant

jokes.text: All my children were taken away.
jokes.setup: Angie mentions needing a nanny after Barbara's death
jokes.punchline: Randi cheerfully reveals her children were taken away as qualification
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly delivered with cheerful obliviousness - classic 30 Rock character comedy

jokes.text: He controls me!
jokes.setup: Someone being told to control their dog
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - the dog controls the owner
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective role reversal, economical delivery

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'chic'. It's French.
jokes.setup: Jack mispronounces her fashion line as 'cheek'
jokes.punchline: Angie's pretentious correction reveals her own mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Angie's pretension and ignorance combined

jokes.text: Reality star, actress, singer/songreader, perfumist, IBS survivor, best-selling author of a book I didn't write, catchphrase coiner
jokes.setup: Angie claims to be an 'octuple threat'
jokes.punchline: List includes increasingly ridiculous 'talents' including IBS survival
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with 'songreader' and 'IBS survivor' as career achievements - pure 30 Rock absurdism

jokes.text: Well they don't all work.
jokes.setup: Angie proudly claims to be a catchphrase coiner
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that her catchphrases don't work
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Angie's delusion meets reality

jokes.text: Cheek is stretchable formal wear for elegant plus-sized women and huskier gays
jokes.setup: Describing her fashion line's target demographic
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and absurd target market including 'huskier gays'
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific demographic targeting that's both offensive and character-appropriate

jokes.text: I find that my target customer sweats a lot and often gets thrown into a public pool
jokes.setup: Explaining design philosophy for her clothing line
jokes.punchline: Assumes customers regularly get thrown into pools
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd assumption about her customers' lifestyles - peak 30 Rock weirdness

jokes.text: a crate of Hollister sweatshirts and a signed headshot of Don Johnson
jokes.setup: Describing what North Korea wants in prisoner exchange
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous items alongside spy exchange
jokes.explanation: Absurd diplomatic demands that feel both random and somehow plausible for 30 Rock universe

jokes.text: They just got Nash Bridges
jokes.setup: Mentioning Don Johnson headshot in prisoner exchange
jokes.punchline: Jack's concern about North Korea's TV viewing options
jokes.explanation: Deadpan delivery treating TV access as diplomatic concern

jokes.text: Excuse me, 'gays' at bravo
jokes.setup: Jack referring to Bravo network employees
jokes.punchline: Correcting himself with stereotypical assumption
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual prejudice played for character comedy

jokes.text: It's my way till pay day
jokes.setup: Context of D'Fwan's boyfriend's pregnancy scare
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical catchphrase that rhymes
jokes.explanation: Typical reality TV catchphrase - meaningless but memorable

jokes.text: Not to be racist, but white guys are typically punctual
jokes.setup: Prefacing with 'not to be racist'
jokes.punchline: Follows with stereotype about white people
jokes.explanation: Subverts 'not to be racist' trope with reverse stereotype

jokes.text: I'm sorry I got so real, but nothing's gonna change unless there's a dialogue
jokes.setup: Following up the racist comment
jokes.punchline: Treating casual racism as meaningful social commentary
jokes.explanation: Mocks reality TV's pretense of addressing social issues

jokes.text: And I've gone to and worked at the post office
jokes.setup: Angie claiming she's never been so disrespected
jokes.punchline: Post office as pinnacle of disrespect
jokes.explanation: Universal understanding of post office frustration as ultimate disrespect

jokes.text: That man is about to get some cheese with that
jokes.setup: Angie being stood up by Jack
jokes.punchline: Attempting to use her failed catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Callback to failed catchphrase attempts - character consistency

jokes.text: That catchphrase is improving, baby!
jokes.setup: Angie's mangled catchphrase attempt
jokes.punchline: Encouraging obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Supportive delusion about obviously terrible catchphrase

jokes.text: Her name is Raven-Symone senior
jokes.setup: Claiming to have a girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous name playing on celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity name mashup with 'senior' suffix

jokes.text: I will be on the show a lot
jokes.setup: Liz predicting she'll be pulled into drama
jokes.punchline: Immediate repetition with more emphasis
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about screen time, slightly forced

jokes.text: Boob monster!
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Jack's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Random insulting outburst
jokes.explanation: Random insult without much setup or payoff

jokes.text: I know they're not married. I just like them to know I don't give a about their lives
jokes.setup: Angie asking if Liz knows where her husband is
jokes.punchline: Admission she knows they're not married but wants to be hurtful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angie moment - calculated cruelty delivered with honesty

jokes.text: Do you know what a surprise is? Now you do
jokes.setup: Liz asking what Tracy's surprise is
jokes.punchline: Angie's illogical explanation of surprises
jokes.explanation: Angie's confident delivery of complete nonsense

jokes.text: Don't learn to talk. A woman's power comes from her silence
jokes.setup: Talking to baby Virginia
jokes.punchline: Anti-feminist advice from someone who never stops talking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - the loudest character advising silence

jokes.text: I thought it was very brave of you to pose for playboy against playboy's wishes
jokes.setup: Liz complimenting Randi on last week's episode
jokes.punchline: The magazine didn't want her to pose
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment structure with great economy

jokes.text: Tracy tells me that you're, you know, the village bicycle
jokes.setup: Randi needing a date to the fashion show
jokes.punchline: Casually delivering harsh insult about Liz
jokes.explanation: Classic insult delivered with reality TV directness

jokes.text: Doctor guy, pilot guy, Cleveland dude, British guy, rich dude, James Franco. I've been with the same woman for 22 years. No judgments, but to me, Liz Lemon is a sex maniac
jokes.setup: Tracy listing Liz's relationships
jokes.punchline: Calling her a sex maniac from perspective of faithful husband
jokes.explanation: Perfect perspective joke - faithful Tracy judging normal dating as promiscuous

jokes.text: Contractually, I can only hold beautiful black babies in Benetton ads
jokes.setup: Being asked to watch Virginia
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific contractual limitation
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and problematic contract clause that feels very Jenna

jokes.text: I might bite these fat little legs. I might eat them up, they're so fat and juicy
jokes.setup: Liz expressing affection for baby Virginia
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing baby talk
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Liz's awkward attempt at baby talk

jokes.text: Don't even let Clinton know about this. He and Steve bing will break out their sex plane. It is a sex plane. There aren't even any seats. It's just futons and jacuzzis
jokes.setup: Jack discussing keeping Avery's return secret
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of Clinton's alleged sex plane
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with specific details about celebrity debauchery

jokes.text: like Ken burns's jazz? Disgusting
jokes.setup: Diana asking about reality shows
jokes.punchline: Treating acclaimed documentary as disgusting reality TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliant category error - highbrow documentary as trashy reality TV

jokes.text: D'Fwine. Please d'fwink responsibly
jokes.setup: Jack offering wine to Diana
jokes.punchline: D'Fwan's wine brand with punny tagline
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate pun, somewhat predictable but fits D'Fwan

jokes.text: Now you sound like the cops outside of Jackie o's funeral. But I got in there and sang almost all of hey big spender
jokes.setup: Jack telling Jenna this is family only
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate funeral performance story
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Jenna story with specific details that make it worse

jokes.text: thank you for looking up on Wikipedia that I was conceived on a toilet
jokes.setup: Jack's insult about Florida bathroom
jokes.punchline: Jenna's overly specific and gross backstory
jokes.explanation: Jenna turning insult into overshare opportunity

jokes.text: If a beautiful woman cries and no one hears it, did she waste $700 on crying lessons at Adrian Brody's unaccredited acting school?
jokes.setup: Jenna lamenting that no one wants her in their story
jokes.punchline: Philosophical question about expensive acting classes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical parody with perfect Jenna specificity - Adrian Brody's unaccredited school is inspired

jokes.text: Gus is someone I've gone into business with and Avery wouldn't approve of him. Why would she disapprove? Uh, well, uh, because they dated... At Yale. Gus was a professor
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his business relationship with Gus
jokes.punchline: Revealing increasingly inappropriate teacher-student relationship
jokes.explanation: Escalating inappropriateness as Jack digs himself deeper

jokes.text: It's at 8:00, which is 9:00 am Tokyo time. That's when takashi's parents leave for work and we play mass effect 3 online. Mr. and Mrs. Tanaka think he's at school, but he's playing video games with a drunk adult
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he can't attend fashion show
jokes.punchline: Detailed explanation of enabling truancy in Japan
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and morally questionable gaming relationship with escalating details

jokes.text: Oh, my god. Ned stark is dead?
jokes.setup: In middle of conversation with Angie
jokes.punchline: Tracy's distracted Game of Thrones spoiler reaction
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference as character moment of distraction

jokes.text: I love my wife. I want her to be happy. But more than that, I'm gonna do nothing. It's hard trying to have it all
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about marriage
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing laziness over love, claims difficulty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - doing nothing is somehow difficult

jokes.text: We're higher up here, so the sun's electricity is stronger
jokes.setup: Jack asking why Gus wears a hat
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of how sun/electricity works
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Gus's odd worldview

jokes.text: She was nasty. She loved pee
jokes.setup: Discussion of Gus and Avery's relationship
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate overshare about past relationship
jokes.explanation: Shocking TMI moment that's both disgusting and character-appropriate

jokes.text: In slavic myth, Rus is the founder of Russia
jokes.setup: Diana trying to explain the Gus/Rus confusion
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily academic explanation for restaurant name
jokes.explanation: Overly intellectual explanation for simple cover story

jokes.text: Who doesn't love cold, purple soup?
jokes.setup: Describing Russian cuisine restaurant
jokes.punchline: Unappealing description of borscht
jokes.explanation: Rhetorical question highlighting unappetizing food

jokes.text: Oh, that is a bridge too far. That's right. I read world war ll history, mother-
jokes.setup: Angie upset about Jack's restaurant opening same night
jokes.punchline: WWII reference with aborted profanity
jokes.explanation: Angie's pretentious historical reference followed by reality TV impulse

jokes.text: This is very, very easy for me to say to you, but you can't come in here right now
jokes.setup: Liz trying to visit Virginia with a gift
jokes.punchline: D'Fwan's contradictory statement about ease/difficulty
jokes.explanation: D'Fwan's illogical phrasing while being dramatic

jokes.text: You never talk about a black woman's leg size. Not on babies, not on the Williams sisters, not on a mannequin at Avenue. We have come too far
jokes.setup: Liz's innocent comment about baby's legs
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic social justice speech about baby compliments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from innocent baby talk to civil rights progress

jokes.text: Lisa, D'Fwan doesn't talk about people behind their backs, so I'm just gonna tell you straight to your face that what you're saying right now is very boring
jokes.setup: Liz opening up about motherhood feelings
jokes.punchline: D'Fwan's brutal honesty while claiming moral high ground
jokes.explanation: Perfect D'Fwan moment - claiming not to gossip while being brutally direct

jokes.text: You people are savages!
jokes.setup: Restaurant preparation chaos
jokes.punchline: Gus's over-the-top reaction to flower arrangements
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Gus's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: I still have to get the menus from the printer, go to the cony island beet market. I've gotta pick up Yakov Smirnov at the airport
jokes.setup: Jack listing restaurant opening tasks
jokes.punchline: Increasingly Russian-themed and ridiculous requirements
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity culminating in dated Russian comedian

jokes.text: You're talking about electricity
jokes.setup: Jack discussing complicated restaurant opening feelings
jokes.punchline: Gus's complete misunderstanding of metaphor
jokes.explanation: Callback to Gus's odd relationship with electricity concepts

jokes.text: Do you act? Because we should do a movie together where we're on a road trip just being sluts
jokes.setup: Jenna talking to baby Virginia
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate movie pitch to infant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - completely inappropriate career advice to baby

jokes.text: I see. I think I just solved the mystery of the phantom pooper
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying bathroom is usually Lutz's office
jokes.punchline: Tracy connecting dots about mysterious bathroom incidents
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace mystery callback with good economy

jokes.text: I wouldn't know. I really don't watch TV. I'm more of a masturbator
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about reality TV behavior
jokes.punchline: Tracy's casual overshare about entertainment preferences
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy TMI delivered with complete casualness

jokes.text: Everything I know about fashion, I learned from my old college roommate, John Mark Karr
jokes.setup: Kenneth being complimented on his fashion sense
jokes.punchline: Reference to infamous criminal as fashion mentor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Kenneth backstory reference - John Mark Karr was suspected in JonBenet Ramsey case

jokes.text: You have no class, you prostitution whore
jokes.setup: Jenna confronting Liz
jokes.punchline: Reality TV style insult with redundant terminology
jokes.explanation: Generic reality TV insult, saved by Jenna's delivery

jokes.text: Like when I sang at that children's hospital. Get away from me, I'm revoking my waiver. Now blur my face. Blur it more. More. Now disguise my voice
jokes.setup: Jenna deciding she's too good for the show
jokes.punchline: Escalating demands for anonymity while staying on camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - wanting fame and anonymity simultaneously with escalating demands

jokes.text: There's the feud between Ms. Lemon and baby Virginia, the drama between me and that cord I tripped on. Hey, watch it. I'm sorry, sir. I shouldn't have lost my temper
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing various dramas
jokes.punchline: Treating inanimate cord as equal participant in drama, then apologizing to it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth moment - treating cord with same seriousness as human drama, then showing characteristic politeness

jokes.text: Oh, I was my grandmother's favorite so I got all her old hand-me-downs
jokes.setup: Size 12 dress needs wearer
jokes.punchline: Gus wearing grandmother's dress as solution
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Gus in dress with sweet explanation about grandmother

jokes.text: What's cocaine like?
jokes.setup: After fashion show chaos
jokes.punchline: Random drug inquiry
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur without much setup or payoff

jokes.text: Portia reads the papers. I hate that that's my catchphrase
jokes.setup: Portia demonstrating knowledge of current events
jokes.punchline: Self-aware complaint about terrible catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on forced reality TV catchphrases

jokes.text: If you don't kiss someone, they're offended
jokes.setup: Jack claiming his kiss with Diana was European custom
jokes.punchline: Weak excuse for inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard excuse-making humor, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: She misjudged you, and she would like a hug
jokes.setup: D'Fwan interpreting baby Virginia's behavior
jokes.punchline: Claiming baby requested specific reconciliation
jokes.explanation: D'Fwan's dramatic interpretation of baby behavior

jokes.text: Because Virginia loves her little giraffe so much, the show is sending us on a girls' getaway to Somalia!
jokes.setup: D'Fwan announcing a trip reward
jokes.punchline: Extremely dangerous location presented as vacation prize
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Somalia as vacation destination delivered with reality TV enthusiasm

jokes.text: I gave you a kidney, a kneecap and a bladder
jokes.setup: Angie listing sacrifices for Tracy
jokes.punchline: Impossible medical donations
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of marital sacrifices including non-transferable body parts

jokes.text: I handle conflict appropriately, and I'm up to date on my mortgage payments
jokes.setup: Daphne defending herself
jokes.punchline: Boring adult responsibilities as character traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to reality TV drama - normal adult behavior as rebellion

jokes.text: We're a train wreck you cannot look away from!
jokes.setup: Tracy and Angie's fake fight
jokes.punchline: Self-aware description of reality TV appeal
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on reality TV while performing it

jokes.text: Lizard egg!
jokes.setup: Tracy and Angie's escalating fake fight
jokes.punchline: Random nonsensical insult
jokes.explanation: Random Tracy insult that's memorable for its weirdness

jokes.text: Both: I'm cheating on you!
jokes.setup: Fake marital fight reaching climax
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous identical revelation
jokes.explanation: Synchronized confession as climax of fake drama

jokes.text: You again? I am so sorry. Different cord
jokes.setup: Kenneth encountering another cord
jokes.punchline: Continuation of cord feud with different opponent
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback extending Kenneth's polite relationship with inanimate objects

tmdb_overview: Angie brings "Queen of Jordan'' back to TGS. News about Avery forces Jack to confront his feelings for her mother, and Liz finds herself in a feud.
insight: 30 Rock's absurdist sequel sustains 86 points across 67 jokes through relentless character escalation.
